segments.text: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the LORD:
I will sing to the LORD, for He is highly exalted. The horse and riderHe has thrown into the sea.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The LORD is my strength and my song, and He has become my salvation. He is my God, and I will praise Him, my father’s God, and I will exalt Him.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The LORD is a warrior, the LORD is His name.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: Pharaoh’s chariots and armyHe has cast into the sea; the finest of his officersare drowned in the Red Sea.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The depths have covered them; they sank there like a stone.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: Your right hand, O LORD, is majestic in power; Your right hand, O LORD, has shattered the enemy.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: You overthrew Your adversariesby Your great majesty. You unleashed Your burning wrath; it consumed them like stubble.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: At the blast of Your nostrilsthe waters piled up; like a wall the currents stood firm; the depths congealed in the heart of the sea.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The enemy declared,‘I will pursue, I will overtake. I will divide the spoils; I will gorge myself on them. I will draw my sword; my hand will destroy them.’
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: But You blew with Your breath, and the sea covered them. They sank like leadin the mighty waters.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: Who among the gods is like You, O LORD? Who is like You—majestic in holiness, revered with praises, performing wonders?
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: You stretched out Your right hand, and the earth swallowed them up.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: With loving devotion You will leadthe people You have redeemed; with Your strength You will guide themto Your holy dwelling.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The nations will hear and tremble; anguish will grip the dwellers of Philistia.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: Then the chiefs of Edom will be dismayed; trembling will seize the leaders of Moab; those who dwell in Canaan will melt away,
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: and terror and dread will fall on them. By the power of Your armthey will be as still as a stoneuntil Your people pass by, O LORD, until the people You have bought pass by.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: You will bring them in and plant themon the mountain of Your inheritance—the place, O LORD, You have prepared for Your dwelling, the sanctuary, O Lord, Your hands have established.
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: The LORD will reign forever and ever!
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text: For when Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, and horsemen went into the sea, the LORD brought the waters of the sea back over them. But the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground.
Then Miriam the prophetess, Aaron’s sister, took a tambourine in her hand, and all the women followed her with tambourines and dancing.
And Miriam sang back to them:
Sing to the LORD, for He is highly exalted; the horse and riderHe has thrown into the sea.
segments.speaker: Miriam

segments.text: Then Moses led Israel from the Red Sea, and they went out into the Desert of Shur. For three days they walked in the desert without finding water.
And when they came to Marah, they could not drink the water there because it was bitter. (That is why it was named Marah.)
So the people grumbled against Moses, saying,
What are we to drink?
segments.speaker: The Israelites

segments.text:
And Moses cried out to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a log. And when he cast it into the waters, they were sweetened.
There the LORD made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there He tested them,
saying,

If you will listen carefully to the voice of the LORD your God, and do what is right in His eyes, and pay attention to His commands, and keep all His statutes, then I will not bring on you any of the diseases I inflicted on the Egyptians. For I am the LORD who heals you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there by the waters.
